_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the model set. Drives the app (API/worker/dashboard) AND the Helm chart (infra/helm/llm-server) that generates per-model k8s resources. Add a model = add an entry here, then ./infra/scripts/deploy-k8s.sh. Set enabled:false to stop serving a model (Helm prunes its resources, the API drops it from /config + scaling). stream / consumer_group / llm_url are DERIVED from id (llm:work:{id}, llm-workers-{id}, http://llm-{id}:8080). model_url is where the GGUF is fetched into the model PVC on first boot. SLOT invariant: keep max_replicas*worker_concurrency <= max_llm_pods*parallel so workers never oversubscribe llama.cpp slots. CPU invariant: keep llm_args.threads == resources.llm.requests.cpu so the k8s scheduler enforces the node's core budget — a pod won't schedule unless that many cores are free, which is what stops two models' KEDA ceilings from oversubscribing CPU. Reserve ~1/4 of physical cores for OS/k8s/redis/workers (don't size the sum of all maxes to 100% of cores).

models._role: ingestion / summary — planned for the 128GB / 32-core prod server (CPU-only). enabled:false until then. 32-core profile: threads 4 == cpu request 4 (honest request → scheduler enforces the core budget). Recommended steady-state ~2 replicas (8 cores) alongside 2x qwen3-32b; KEDA ceiling 4.
models.label: Qwen3-8B

models._role: orchestrator / analysis — planned for the 128GB / 32-core prod server (CPU-only). enabled:false until then. ~23GB GGUF (Q5_K_M). 32-core profile: threads 8 == cpu request 8 (honest request → scheduler enforces the core budget). Recommended steady-state ~2 replicas (16 cores) alongside 2x qwen3-8b = 24 cores, leaving ~8 for OS/k8s/redis/workers; KEDA ceiling 3.
models.label: Qwen3-32B
